gate_rows.next_proof_artifact: Embedding supply-chain experiment report with model revision/checksum, local runtime envelope, vector dimensions, and no-secret/no-raw-vector leakage checks.
non_claims: Does not promote any embedding model to product default.
Does not allow managed embedding API fallback.
Does not prove retrieval quality.
gate_rows.rationale: Embedding candidates need local model provenance, integrity, resource, and leakage checks before promotion.
gate_rows.title: Embedding model supply-chain integrity gate

gate_rows.next_proof_artifact: Temporal conflict policy plus fixture-backed same-date/new-edition/source-revision tests.
non_claims: Does not specify temporal storage implementation.
Does not validate temporal conflict resolution.
Does not prove import runtime behavior.
gate_rows.rationale: Same-date temporal conflict behavior is a product semantics decision before runtime fixture proof can be meaningful.
gate_rows.title: Temporal same-date multi-edition conflict policy

gate_rows.next_proof_artifact: Parser/retrieval golden-test suite using tracked parser records and explicit no-answer/non-claim cases.
non_claims: Does not prove parser completeness.
Does not prove citation-safe retrieval.
Does not prove legal-answer correctness.
gate_rows.rationale: Parser and retrieval proof already has bounded M006 artifacts; the next useful step is executable golden tests over real parser outputs and expected retrieval/evidence behavior.
gate_rows.title: Product parser and retrieval readiness gate

gate_rows.next_proof_artifact: Retrieval quality and migration experiment report with dataset skeleton, metrics, fallback boundaries, and explicit non-readiness result states.
non_claims: Does not prove product retrieval quality.
Does not commit to a vector store topology.
Does not validate FalkorDB production scale.
gate_rows.rationale: Retrieval quality and migration strategy need measured runtime/evaluation evidence rather than registry-only assertions.
gate_rows.title: Local embedding quality proof

gate_rows.next_proof_artifact: Runtime migration smoke proof with import package, graph load shape, rollback/failure diagnostics, and non-production boundary.
non_claims: Does not prove FalkorDB production loading behavior.
Does not prove production scale.
Does not prove product ETL readiness.
gate_rows.rationale: Runtime migration/readiness claims require executable import/load/migration smoke evidence, not registry coverage alone.
gate_rows.title: FalkorDBLite to Docker migration runbook

gate_rows.next_proof_artifact: Generated-Cypher validator suite covering read-only enforcement, schema grounding, evidence-returning queries, injection rejection, and unsafe write rejection.
non_claims: Does not prove provider generation quality.
Does not authorize executing raw generated Cypher.
Does not validate product Legal KnowQL readiness.
gate_rows.rationale: Generated-Cypher safety is the highest-risk explicit gate and should be retired by deterministic validator tests before any Legal KnowQL product-readiness claim.
gate_rows.title: Generated-Cypher safety and validation gate

gate_rows.next_proof_artifact: Legal Nexus access-control decision and negative-test plan defining caller roles, authorization boundaries, audit logging, and denied-operation diagnostics.
non_claims: Does not assert current product is insecure.
Does not prove access-control enforcement.
Does not define a production API surface.
gate_rows.rationale: Access-control proof needs a product API/caller boundary decision before meaningful authz tests can be specified.
gate_rows.title: Legal Nexus access-control proof gate

non_claims: This matrix is a planning artifact, not product readiness evidence.
Disposition classes prioritize proof work; they do not validate runtime behavior.
Open proof gates remain unresolved until their next_proof_artifact is produced and verified.
Resolving all R04 recommendations does not prove legal-answer correctness, parser completeness, retrieval quality, generated-Cypher safety, FalkorDB production behavior, or LLM legal authority.

recommendation_rows.next: Keep as coverage baseline; do not treat one record per layer as completeness.
non_claims: Does not assert that one record per empty layer makes the layer complete.
Does not assert that the added records prove product behavior.
recommendation_rows.title: Populate three empty schema layers

recommendation_rows.next: Keep connectivity visible in graph report; reassess if new orphan findings appear.
non_claims: Does not assert that current orphans contain incorrect content.
Does not require using unused edge types as a goal in itself.
recommendation_rows.title: Connect orphan nodes via existing edge types
